name: channels

metadata.name: channels
metadata.summary: Brings async, event-driven capabilities to Django.

documentation:
# Django Channels

```{image} https://github.com/django/channels/workflows/Tests/badge.svg?branch=master
:target: https://github.com/django/channels/actions
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/channels/badge/?version=latest
:target: https://channels.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/channels.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/channels
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/channels.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/channels
```

Channels augments Django to bring WebSocket, long-poll HTTP,
task offloading and other async support to your code, using familiar Django
design patterns and a flexible underlying framework that lets you not only
customize behaviours but also write support for your own protocols and needs.

Documentation, installation and getting started instructions are at
<https://channels.readthedocs.io>

Channels is an official Django Project and as such has a deprecation policy.
Details about what's deprecated or pending deprecation for each release is in
the [release notes](https://channels.readthedocs.io/en/latest/releases/index.html).

Support can be obtained through several locations - see our
[support docs](https://channels.readthedocs.io/en/latest/support.html) for more.

You can install channels from PyPI as the `channels` package.
See our [installation](https://channels.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation.html)
and [tutorial](https://channels.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tutorial/index.html) docs for more.

## Dependencies

All Channels projects currently support Python 3.8 and up. `channels` is
compatible with Django 4.2 and 5.0.

## Contributing

To learn more about contributing, please [read our contributing docs](https://channels.readthedocs.io/en/latest/contributing.html).

## Maintenance and Security

To report security issues, please contact <mailto:security@djangoproject.com>. For GPG
signatures and more security process information, see
<https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/dev/internals/security/>.

To report bugs or request new features, please open a new GitHub issue. For
larger discussions, please post to the
[django-developers mailing list](https://groups.google.com/d/forum/django-developers).

Maintenance is overseen by Carlton Gibson with help from others. It is a
best-effort basis - we unfortunately can only dedicate guaranteed time to fixing
security holes.

If you are interested in joining the maintenance team, please
[read more about contributing](https://channels.readthedocs.io/en/latest/contributing.html)
and get in touch!

## Other Projects

The Channels project is made up of several packages; the others are:

- [Daphne](https://github.com/django/daphne/), the HTTP and Websocket termination server
- [channels_redis](https://github.com/django/channels_redis/), the Redis channel backend
- [asgiref](https://github.com/django/asgiref/), the base ASGI library/memory backend

functions.name: channels.utils.name_that_thing
documentation: Returns either the function/class path or just the object's repr

functions.name: channels.utils.await_many_dispatch
documentation:
Given a set of consumer callables, awaits on them all and passes results
from them to the dispatch awaitable as they come in.

classes.name: channels.testing.http.HttpCommunicator

methods.name: channels.testing.http.HttpCommunicator.get_response
documentation:
Get the application's response. Returns a dict with keys of
"body", "headers" and "status".

ApplicationCommunicator subclass that has HTTP shortcut methods.

It will construct the scope for you, so you need to pass the application
(uninstantiated) along with HTTP parameters.

This does not support full chunking - for that, just use ApplicationCommunicator
directly.

classes.name: channels.testing.websocket.WebsocketCommunicator

methods.name: channels.testing.websocket.WebsocketCommunicator.connect
documentation:
Trigger the connection code.

On an accepted connection, returns (True, <chosen-subprotocol>)
On a rejected connection, returns (False, <close-code>)

methods.name: channels.testing.websocket.WebsocketCommunicator.send_to
documentation: Sends a WebSocket frame to the application.

methods.name: channels.testing.websocket.WebsocketCommunicator.send_json_to
documentation: Sends JSON data as a text frame

methods.name: channels.testing.websocket.WebsocketCommunicator.receive_from
documentation:
Receives a data frame from the view. Will fail if the connection
closes instead. Returns either a bytestring or a unicode string
depending on what sort of frame you got.

methods.name: channels.testing.websocket.WebsocketCommunicator.receive_json_from
documentation: Receives a JSON text frame payload and decodes it

methods.name: channels.testing.websocket.WebsocketCommunicator.disconnect
documentation: Closes the socket

ApplicationCommunicator subclass that has WebSocket shortcut methods.

It will construct the scope for you, so you need to pass the application
(uninstantiated) along with the initial connection parameters.

functions.name: channels.layers.get_channel_layer
documentation: Returns a channel layer by alias, or None if it is not configured.

classes.name: channels.layers.ChannelLayerManager

methods.name: channels.layers.ChannelLayerManager._reset_backends
documentation: Removes cached channel layers when the CHANNEL_LAYERS setting changes.

methods.name: channels.layers.ChannelLayerManager.make_backend
documentation: Instantiate channel layer.

methods.name: channels.layers.ChannelLayerManager.make_test_backend
documentation: Instantiate channel layer using its test config.

methods.name: channels.layers.ChannelLayerManager.set
documentation:
Sets an alias to point to a new ChannelLayerWrapper instance, and
returns the old one that it replaced. Useful for swapping out the
backend during tests.
Takes a settings dictionary of backends and initialises them on request.

classes.name: channels.layers.BaseChannelLayer

methods.name: channels.layers.BaseChannelLayer.compile_capacities
documentation:
Takes an input channel_capacity dict and returns the compiled list
of regexes that get_capacity will look for as self.channel_capacity

methods.name: channels.layers.BaseChannelLayer.get_capacity
documentation:
Gets the correct capacity for the given channel; either the default,
or a matching result from channel_capacity. Returns the first matching
result; if you want to control the order of matches, use an ordered dict
as input.

methods.name: channels.layers.BaseChannelLayer.non_local_name
documentation:
Given a channel name, returns the "non-local" part. If the channel name
is a process-specific channel (contains !) this means the part up to
and including the !; if it is anything else, this means the full name.

Base channel layer class that others can inherit from, with useful
common functionality.

classes.name: channels.layers.InMemoryChannelLayer

methods.name: channels.layers.InMemoryChannelLayer.send
documentation: Send a message onto a (general or specific) channel.

methods.name: channels.layers.InMemoryChannelLayer.receive
documentation:
Receive the first message that arrives on the channel.
If more than one coroutine waits on the same channel, a random one
of the waiting coroutines will get the result.

methods.name: channels.layers.InMemoryChannelLayer.new_channel
documentation:
Returns a new channel name that can be used by something in our
process as a specific channel.

methods.name: channels.layers.InMemoryChannelLayer._clean_expired
documentation:
Goes through all messages and groups and removes those that are expired.
Any channel with an expired message is removed from all groups.
Expire cleanup

methods.name: channels.layers.InMemoryChannelLayer.flush
documentation: Flush extension

methods.name: channels.layers.InMemoryChannelLayer._remove_from_groups
documentation: Removes a channel from all groups. Used when a message on it expires.

methods.name: channels.layers.InMemoryChannelLayer.group_add
documentation: Adds the channel name to a group.
Groups extension
In-memory channel layer implementation

classes.name: channels.testing.live.ChannelsLiveServerTestCase

methods.name: channels.testing.live.ChannelsLiveServerTestCase._is_in_memory_db
documentation: Check if DatabaseWrapper holds in memory database.

Does basically the same as TransactionTestCase but also launches a
live Daphne server in a separate process, so
that the tests may use another test framework, such as Selenium,
instead of the built-in dummy client.

classes.name: channels.exceptions.RequestAborted
documentation:
Raised when the incoming request tells us it's aborted partway through
reading the body.

classes.name: channels.exceptions.RequestTimeout
documentation: Aborted specifically due to timeout.

classes.name: channels.exceptions.InvalidChannelLayerError
documentation: Raised when a channel layer is configured incorrectly.

classes.name: channels.exceptions.AcceptConnection
documentation:
Raised during a websocket.connect (or other supported connection) handler
to accept the connection.

classes.name: channels.exceptions.DenyConnection
documentation:
Raised during a websocket.connect (or other supported connection) handler
to deny the connection.

classes.name: channels.exceptions.ChannelFull
documentation: Raised when a channel cannot be sent to as it is over capacity.

classes.name: channels.exceptions.MessageTooLarge
documentation: Raised when a message cannot be sent as it's too big.

classes.name: channels.exceptions.StopConsumer
documentation: Raised when a consumer wants to stop and close down its application instance.

functions.name: channels.consumer.get_handler_name
documentation:
Looks at a message, checks it has a sensible type, and returns the
handler name for that type.

classes.name: channels.consumer.AsyncConsumer

methods.name: channels.consumer.AsyncConsumer.__call__
documentation: Dispatches incoming messages to type-based handlers asynchronously.

methods.name: channels.consumer.AsyncConsumer.dispatch
documentation: Works out what to do with a message.

methods.name: channels.consumer.AsyncConsumer.send
documentation: Overrideable/callable-by-subclasses send method.

methods.name: channels.consumer.AsyncConsumer.as_asgi
documentation:
Return an ASGI v3 single callable that instantiates a consumer instance
per scope. Similar in purpose to Django's as_view().

initkwargs will be used to instantiate the consumer instance.

Base consumer class. Implements the ASGI application spec, and adds on
channel layer management and routing of events to named methods based
on their type.

classes.name: channels.consumer.SyncConsumer

methods.name: channels.consumer.SyncConsumer.dispatch
documentation: Dispatches incoming messages to type-based handlers asynchronously.

methods.name: channels.consumer.SyncConsumer.send
documentation: Overrideable/callable-by-subclasses send method.

Synchronous version of the consumer, which is what we write most of the
generic consumers against (for now). Calls handlers in a threadpool and
uses CallBouncer to get the send method out to the main event loop.

It would have been possible to have "mixed" consumers and auto-detect
if a handler was awaitable or not, but that would have made the API
for user-called methods very confusing as there'd be two types of each.

functions.name: channels.sessions.SessionMiddlewareStack
documentation: Shortcut to include cookie middleware

classes.name: channels.sessions.CookieMiddleware

methods.name: channels.sessions.CookieMiddleware.set_cookie
documentation:
Sets a cookie in the passed HTTP response message.

`expires` can be:
\- a string in the correct format,
\- a naive `datetime.datetime` object in UTC,
\- an aware `datetime.datetime` object in any time zone.
If it is a `datetime.datetime` object then `max_age` will be calculated.

methods.name: channels.sessions.CookieMiddleware.delete_cookie
documentation: Deletes a cookie in a response.

Extracts cookies from HTTP or WebSocket-style scopes and adds them as a
scope["cookies"] entry with the same format as Django's request.COOKIES.

classes.name: channels.sessions.InstanceSessionWrapper

methods.name: channels.sessions.InstanceSessionWrapper.send
documentation: Overridden send that also does session saves/cookies.

methods.name: channels.sessions.InstanceSessionWrapper.save_session
documentation: Saves the current session.

Populates the session in application instance scope, and wraps send to save
the session.

classes.name: channels.sessions.SessionMiddleware

methods.name: channels.sessions.SessionMiddleware.__call__
documentation:
Instantiate a session wrapper for this scope, resolve the session and
call the inner application.

Class that adds Django sessions (from HTTP cookies) to the
scope. Works with HTTP or WebSocket protocol types (or anything that
provides a "headers" entry in the scope).

Requires the CookieMiddleware to be higher up in the stack.

classes.name: channels.generic.websocket.WebsocketConsumer

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.WebsocketConsumer.websocket_connect
documentation: Called when a WebSocket connection is opened.

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.WebsocketConsumer.accept
documentation: Accepts an incoming socket

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.WebsocketConsumer.websocket_receive
documentation:
Called when a WebSocket frame is received. Decodes it and passes it
to receive().

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.WebsocketConsumer.receive
documentation: Called with a decoded WebSocket frame.

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.WebsocketConsumer.send
documentation: Sends a reply back down the WebSocket

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.WebsocketConsumer.close
documentation: Closes the WebSocket from the server end

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.WebsocketConsumer.websocket_disconnect
documentation:
Called when a WebSocket connection is closed. Base level so you don't
need to call super() all the time.

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.WebsocketConsumer.disconnect
documentation: Called when a WebSocket connection is closed.

Base WebSocket consumer. Provides a general encapsulation for the
WebSocket handling model that other applications can build on.

classes.name: channels.generic.websocket.JsonWebsocketConsumer

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.JsonWebsocketConsumer.receive_json
documentation: Called with decoded JSON content.

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.JsonWebsocketConsumer.send_json
documentation: Encode the given content as JSON and send it to the client.

Variant of WebsocketConsumer that automatically JSON-encodes and decodes
messages as they come in and go out. Expects everything to be text; will
error on binary data.

classes.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncWebsocketConsumer

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncWebsocketConsumer.websocket_connect
documentation: Called when a WebSocket connection is opened.

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncWebsocketConsumer.accept
documentation: Accepts an incoming socket

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncWebsocketConsumer.websocket_receive
documentation:
Called when a WebSocket frame is received. Decodes it and passes it
to receive().

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncWebsocketConsumer.receive
documentation: Called with a decoded WebSocket frame.

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncWebsocketConsumer.send
documentation: Sends a reply back down the WebSocket

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncWebsocketConsumer.close
documentation: Closes the WebSocket from the server end

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncWebsocketConsumer.websocket_disconnect
documentation:
Called when a WebSocket connection is closed. Base level so you don't
need to call super() all the time.

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncWebsocketConsumer.disconnect
documentation: Called when a WebSocket connection is closed.

Base WebSocket consumer, async version. Provides a general encapsulation
for the WebSocket handling model that other applications can build on.

classes.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncJsonWebsocketConsumer

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncJsonWebsocketConsumer.receive_json
documentation: Called with decoded JSON content.

methods.name: channels.generic.websocket.AsyncJsonWebsocketConsumer.send_json
documentation: Encode the given content as JSON and send it to the client.

Variant of AsyncWebsocketConsumer that automatically JSON-encodes and decodes
messages as they come in and go out. Expects everything to be text; will
error on binary data.

functions.name: channels.auth.get_user
documentation:
Return the user model instance associated with the given scope.
If no user is retrieved, return an instance of `AnonymousUser`.

functions.name: channels.auth.login
documentation:
Persist a user id and a backend in the request.
This way a user doesn't have to re-authenticate on every request.
Note that data set during the anonymous session is retained when the user
logs in.

functions.name: channels.auth.logout
documentation:
Remove the authenticated user's ID from the request and flush their session
data.

functions.name: channels.auth.AuthMiddlewareStack
documentation: Handy shortcut for applying all three layers at once

classes.name: channels.auth.UserLazyObject
documentation:
Throw a more useful error message when scope['user'] is accessed before
it's resolved

classes.name: channels.auth.AuthMiddleware
documentation:
Middleware which populates scope["user"] from a Django session.
Requires SessionMiddleware to function.

functions.name: channels.security.websocket.AllowedHostsOriginValidator
documentation:
Factory function which returns an OriginValidator configured to use
settings.ALLOWED_HOSTS.

classes.name: channels.security.websocket.OriginValidator

methods.name: channels.security.websocket.OriginValidator.valid_origin
documentation:
Checks parsed origin is None.

Pass control to the validate_origin function.

Returns `True` if validation function was successful, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: channels.security.websocket.OriginValidator.validate_origin
documentation:
Validate the given origin for this site.

Check than the origin looks valid and matches the origin pattern in
specified list `allowed_origins`. Any pattern begins with a scheme.
After the scheme there must be a domain. Any domain beginning with a
period corresponds to the domain and all its subdomains (for example,
`http://.example.com`). After the domain there must be a port,
but it can be omitted. `*` matches anything and anything
else must match exactly.

Note. This function assumes that the given origin has a schema, domain
and port, but port is optional.

Returns `True` for a valid host, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: channels.security.websocket.OriginValidator.match_allowed_origin
documentation:
Returns `True` if the origin is either an exact match or a match
to the wildcard pattern. Compares scheme, domain, port of origin and pattern.

Any pattern can be begins with a scheme. After the scheme must be a domain,
or just domain without scheme.
Any domain beginning with a period corresponds to the domain and all
its subdomains (for example, `.example.com` `example.com`
and any subdomain). Also with scheme (for example, `http://.example.com`
`http://exapmple.com`). After the domain there must be a port,
but it can be omitted.

Note. This function assumes that the given origin is either None, a
schema-domain-port string, or just a domain string

methods.name: channels.security.websocket.OriginValidator.get_origin_port
documentation:
Returns the origin.port or port for this schema by default.
Otherwise, it returns None.

Validates that the incoming connection has an Origin header that
is in an allowed list.

classes.name: channels.security.websocket.WebsocketDenier
documentation: Simple application which denies all requests to it.

classes.name: channels.db.DatabaseSyncToAsync
documentation: SyncToAsync version that cleans up old database connections when it exits.

classes.name: channels.generic.http.AsyncHttpConsumer

methods.name: channels.generic.http.AsyncHttpConsumer.send_headers
documentation:
Sets the HTTP response status and headers. Headers may be provided as
a list of tuples or as a dictionary.

Note that the ASGI spec requires that the protocol server only starts
sending the response to the client after `self.send_body` has been
called the first time.

methods.name: channels.generic.http.AsyncHttpConsumer.send_body
documentation:
Sends a response body to the client. The method expects a bytestring.

Set `more_body=True` if you want to send more body content later.
The default behavior closes the response, and further messages on
the channel will be ignored.

methods.name: channels.generic.http.AsyncHttpConsumer.send_response
documentation:
Sends a response to the client. This is a thin wrapper over
`self.send_headers` and `self.send_body`, and everything said
above applies here as well. This method may only be called once.

methods.name: channels.generic.http.AsyncHttpConsumer.handle
documentation:
Receives the request body as a bytestring. Response may be composed
using the `self.send*` methods; the return value of this method is
thrown away.

methods.name: channels.generic.http.AsyncHttpConsumer.disconnect
documentation:
Overrideable place to run disconnect handling. Do not send anything
from here.

methods.name: channels.generic.http.AsyncHttpConsumer.http_request
documentation:
Async entrypoint - concatenates body fragments and hands off control
to `self.handle` when the body has been completely received.

methods.name: channels.generic.http.AsyncHttpConsumer.http_disconnect
documentation: Let the user do their cleanup and close the consumer.

Async HTTP consumer. Provides basic primitives for building asynchronous
HTTP endpoints.

classes.name: channels.worker.Worker

methods.name: channels.worker.Worker.handle
documentation: Listens on all the provided channels and handles the messages.

methods.name: channels.worker.Worker.listener
documentation: Single-channel listener

ASGI protocol server that surfaces events sent to specific channels
on the channel layer into a single application instance.

classes.name: channels.middleware.BaseMiddleware

methods.name: channels.middleware.BaseMiddleware.__init__
documentation: Middleware constructor - just takes inner application.

methods.name: channels.middleware.BaseMiddleware.__call__
documentation:
ASGI application; can insert things into the scope and run asynchronous
code.

Base class for implementing ASGI middleware.

Note that subclasses of this are not self-safe; don't store state on
the instance, as it serves multiple application instances. Instead, use
scope.

functions.name: channels.routing.get_default_application
documentation: Gets the default application, set in the ASGI_APPLICATION setting.

classes.name: channels.routing.ProtocolTypeRouter
documentation:
Takes a mapping of protocol type names to other Application instances,
and dispatches to the right one based on protocol name (or raises an error)

classes.name: channels.routing.URLRouter
documentation:
Routes to different applications/consumers based on the URL path.

Works with anything that has a `path` key, but intended for WebSocket
and HTTP. Uses Django's django.urls objects for resolution -
path() or re_path().

classes.name: channels.routing.ChannelNameRouter
documentation:
Maps to different applications based on a "channel" key in the scope
(intended for the Channels worker mode)
